telegram.title: Telegram notifications
telegram.description: Get leave decisions, shift assignments, and daily summaries delivered to your personal Telegram chat. This is separate from any workspace group chat your owner sets up.
telegram.notConfigured: Bot is not configured on the server
telegram.notConfiguredHint: Ask your administrator to set TELEGRAM_BOT_USERNAME.
telegram.waitingHint: After you tap Start in Telegram, we'll detect the connection automatically.
telegram.timeout: Timed out waiting for Telegram. Try again.

onboarding.subtitle: Staff attendance, brewed simply
onboarding.ownerDesc: Create your workspace and manage staff
onboarding.idExplain: Share this ID with your employer so they can add you as an employee.
onboarding.linkExplain: Enter the employee ID provided by your employer.

dashboard.emptySubtitle: Your restaurant is all set up. Let's add your team and get attendance tracking started.
dashboard.emptyAddEmployeeDesc: Create a profile and QR code for check-in
dashboard.emptyCreateShiftDesc: Define morning, evening, or custom hours
dashboard.emptyConfigureDesc: Set timezone, IP restriction, and more
dashboard.summaryEmpty: Get started by adding your first employee and creating a shift.
dashboard.summaryAllPresent_one: All 1 employee is present today. Great job!
dashboard.summaryAllPresent_other: All {{count}} employees are present today. Great job!
dashboard.summaryNonePresent_one: None of your 1 employee has checked in yet today.
dashboard.summaryNonePresent_other: None of your {{count}} employees have checked in yet today.
dashboard.noUpcomingLeaves: No upcoming leaves in the next 2 weeks
dashboard.upcomingLeavesUpsell: See who's taking time off in the next 2 weeks. Upgrade to Espresso to manage leave requests.
dashboard.checkinQrDesc: Print and display this at your restaurant. Employees scan it with the DailyBrew app to check in and out.

employee.name: Name
employee.managerPermissionsDesc: Choose which areas this manager can administer. Workspace settings, billing, sub-QR codes and promoting other managers stay with the owner.
employee.permEmployeesDesc: Create, edit, and remove employees (cannot promote managers)
employee.permShiftsDesc: Create, edit, and remove shifts and per-day overrides
employee.permClosuresDesc: Create, edit, and remove restaurant closure dates
employee.permLeaveDesc: Approve, reject, and cancel leave requests for any employee
employee.permAttendanceDesc: View all attendance and edit records when corrections are needed
employee.attendanceTrackingHint: Set "Excluded" for staff who help run the workspace but don't follow a shift (e.g. admin helpers). They can still check in to log times — they just won't be counted as absent.
employee.roleManagerHint: Managers need a linked user account. Link one before saving.
employee.createdGuideTitle: Next, help this employee check in
employee.createdGuideDescription: Finish the setup by linking their user account and sharing the check-in instructions before their first shift.
employee.createdGuideLinkDesc: Ask the employee to sign in and send their user public ID, or share this employee ID so they can link during onboarding.
employee.createdGuideShareTitle: Share the employee ID or QR
employee.createdGuideShareDesc: The QR code and employee ID below are what staff use to connect their account to this profile.
employee.createdGuideShiftMissing: No shift is assigned yet. Add one so late, absent, and early-leave status can be calculated.
employee.createdGuideAdvancedDesc: After staff profiles are ready, tighten check-in rules so attendance is recorded from the right place, network, and device.
employee.createdGuideIpDesc: Only allow check-ins from your restaurant Wi-Fi or approved network.
employee.createdGuideGeofencingDesc: Require staff to be near the restaurant before check-in is accepted.
employee.createdGuideDeviceDesc: Keep check-in and check-out on the same phone to reduce buddy punching.
employee.createdGuideReadFullGuide: Read the full owner setup guide

verification.title: Check-in verification
verification.atRestaurantHint: Check-in is only allowed within the restaurant boundary
verification.sameDeviceHint: Same device for check-in and check-out, and not reused by another employee that day
verification.restaurantNetworkHint: Check-in is only allowed from an approved network
verification.upgradeHint: Upgrade to Espresso to enforce this
verification.upsell: Upgrade to Espresso to verify where, on which device, and from which network staff check in.

settings.geofencingDesc: Only allow check-ins within a set distance from your restaurant
settings.deviceVerificationDesc: Require the same device for check-in and check-out, and prevent one device from checking in multiple employees
settings.nfcCooldownHint: Prevents accidental double-taps. Set to 0 to disable.

checkin.ipRestricted: Check-in not allowed from this location
checkin.locationDenied: Location access denied. Check-in may fail if geofencing is enabled.
checkin.outsideGeofence: You are outside the allowed check-in area
checkin.deviceMismatch: Check-out must be from the same device used for check-in
checkin.deviceAlreadyUsed: This device has already been used to check in another employee today

qrCodes.intro: Create additional QR codes for different sections or floors. Only assigned employees can scan a sub-QR. Settings can be inherited from the workspace or overridden per QR.
qrCodes.name: Name
qrCodes.managerHint: Manager must have a linked user account. They can approve/reject leave for assigned employees.
qrCodes.noEmployees: No employees in this workspace yet.
qrCodes.ipRestrictionDesc: Only allow check-in via this QR from listed network addresses (e.g. your restaurant Wi-Fi). When inheriting, the workspace’s IP rules apply.
qrCodes.geofencingDesc: Require employees to be physically near a coordinate to check in via this QR. Useful for sectioning floors or outdoor areas. Radius minimum is 50 meters.
qrCodes.deviceVerificationDesc: Bind check-in/out to a single device per employee per day. Prevents one phone from punching in multiple employees and forces check-out from the same device used for check-in.
qrCodes.workspaceOn: Currently turned on at the workspace level
qrCodes.workspaceOff: Currently turned off at the workspace level
qrCodes.customForThisQr: Custom rules for this QR — workspace settings are ignored.
qrCodes.deleteConfirm: Delete {{name}}? Employees assigned to it will lose access. This cannot be undone.
qrCodes.planGate: Multiple QR codes require the Double Espresso plan
qrCodes.afterCreateHint: You’ll be taken to the QR detail page to assign employees and adjust settings.

ipRestriction.title: Unlock IP restriction
ipRestriction.description: Restrict QR check-ins to specific IP addresses. Keep attendance accurate by ensuring staff can only check in from your restaurant's network.

geofencing.title: Unlock geofencing
geofencing.description: Set a geographic boundary around your restaurant. Staff can only check in when they are physically nearby.

deviceVerification.title: Unlock device verification
deviceVerification.description: Prevent buddy punching by requiring the same device for check-in and check-out.

leaveRequests.title: Unlock leave requests
leaveRequests.description: Let your staff submit leave requests directly. Review, approve, or reject them from your dashboard.

shiftTimeRules.title: Unlock per-day schedules
shiftTimeRules.description: Override shift hours for specific days of the week. Perfect for restaurants with different hours on weekends.

telegramNotifications.title: Unlock Telegram notifications
telegramNotifications.description: Send real-time leave requests, shift changes, closures, and daily summaries to a Telegram chat or group.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitlePrefix: Everything you need to know about DailyBrew. Can't find what you're looking for?

q.freePlan: What's included in the Free plan?
q.managerDefaults: What are the default manager permissions?
q.managerLimit: How many managers can I have?
q.promoteManager: How do I promote an employee to manager?
q.managerNotAllowed: What can a manager NOT do?
q.oauthSignIn: Can I use Google or Apple to sign in?
q.resetPassword: How do I reset my password?

a.whatIsDailybrew: DailyBrew is a staff attendance tracking platform built for restaurants. It provides QR code check-in, shift management, closure periods, and leave request handling, all in one simple dashboard.
a.whoIsItFor: DailyBrew is designed for restaurant owners, managers, and staff. Owners manage the workspace, shifts, and employees. Managers receive only the permissions you grant them — for example leave approvals, attendance oversight, employee management, or any combination. Staff check in and out by scanning the workspace QR code.
a.mobileApp: Staff check-in works entirely through the mobile web browser. When an employee scans their QR code, a mobile-optimized check-in page opens instantly. No app download is required.
a.freePlan: The Free plan includes up to 10 employees, QR check-in, shift tracking, closure periods, and the attendance dashboard. It is free forever with no time limit.
a.espressoPlan: Espresso supports up to 20 employees and adds leave request management, IP restriction, device verification, geofencing, per-day shift schedules, manager role with granular permissions (up to 2), push and email notifications, and daily attendance summaries.
a.espressoCost: Espresso costs $14.99 per month, or $149 per year if you choose annual billing. The yearly plan saves you $30.88 compared to paying monthly.
a.cancelAnytime: Yes, there are no contracts or lock-in periods. You can cancel your Espresso subscription at any time from your dashboard Settings. After cancellation, you will continue to have access until the end of your current billing period.
a.qrCheckin: Each workspace has a unique QR code. Display it at your restaurant entrance. Staff open the DailyBrew app, scan the QR code, and tap the Check In button to record their arrival. They scan again and tap Check Out when they leave.
a.geofencing: Geofencing restricts check-in to a specific geographic area. You set a center point and a radius, and staff must be physically within that area to check in. This ensures attendance is only recorded when employees are actually at the restaurant.
a.ipRestriction: IP restriction only allows check-in from specific IP addresses, such as your restaurant WiFi network. When enabled, staff scanning the QR code from a different network will see a message explaining they need to be connected to the restaurant network.
a.closures: When you define a closure period (for example, a holiday or renovation), no attendance is expected on those dates. The system skips those days entirely, so employees will not be marked as absent during closures.
a.managerRole: The manager role lets you promote trusted employees and choose exactly which areas they can administer. Each manager has a per-permission toggle for: manage employees, manage shifts, manage closures, manage leave, and manage attendance. You can grant any combination — for example, a head waiter who only handles leave approvals, or a co-runner who manages everything except billing and settings.
a.managerDefaults: Newly promoted managers start with "manage leave" and "manage attendance" enabled — meaning they can approve leave for any employee and view all attendance records. You can flip the other toggles on or off at any time from the employee detail page.
a.managerLimit: On the Espresso plan, you can have up to 2 managers per workspace. The Double Espresso plan allows unlimited managers.
a.promoteManager: Go to the employee detail page and click "Promote to manager". The employee must have a linked user account (they need to sign in to use manager features). Once promoted, a "Manager permissions" card appears below — toggle each capability on or off as needed. You can demote them back to a regular employee at any time.
a.managerNotAllowed: No matter which permissions you grant, managers cannot rename or delete the workspace, edit workspace settings (IP / device / geofencing / timezone), manage billing, mint or edit sub-QR codes, or promote / demote other managers. These remain owner-only.
a.signUp: Click the "Get started" button on the homepage. Create an account with your email and password, then set up your workspace by naming your restaurant. From there you can add employees and generate QR codes.
a.oauthSignIn: Yes, DailyBrew supports both Google and Apple sign-in. You can link your account during registration or from your profile settings later.
a.resetPassword: Click "Forgot password" on the sign-in page. Enter your email address and we will send you a password reset link. The link expires after 1 hour for security.

cta.text: Still have questions? We are happy to help.

index.title: Everything you need to track attendance
index.subtitle: From QR check-in to geofencing — simple tools that work for restaurants of any size.

qrCheckin.title: QR code check-in
qrCheckin.desc: Display one QR code at your restaurant. Staff scan it from the DailyBrew app to check in and out — no extra hardware needed.

employeeManagement.title: Employee management
employeeManagement.desc: Add staff, assign them to shifts, and track their attendance history. Manage active and inactive employees from one screen.

shiftTracking.title: Shift tracking
shiftTracking.desc: Define morning, evening, or custom shifts with start and end times. Late arrivals and early departures are flagged automatically with configurable grace periods.

closurePeriods.title: Closure periods
closurePeriods.desc: Mark holidays, renovations, or any days your restaurant is closed. Attendance is paused automatically — no false absences.

realtimeDashboard.title: Real-time dashboard
realtimeDashboard.desc: See who is present, late, on leave, or absent at a glance. Owner and employee dashboards with live stats and recent history.

multiLanguage.title: Multi-language
multiLanguage.desc: Available in English, French, and Khmer. Staff can use the app in the language they are most comfortable with.

ipRestriction.title: IP restriction
ipRestriction.desc: Lock check-ins to your restaurant's WiFi or network. If staff aren't on an approved IP, they can't clock in — no remote punching.

deviceVerification.title: Device verification
deviceVerification.desc: Each employee must check in and out from the same device per day. Stops buddy punching — a colleague can't clock out on someone else's phone.
highlights: One device per employee per day

geofencing.title: Geofencing
geofencing.desc: Draw a virtual perimeter around your restaurant. Staff must be physically within the GPS radius to check in — configurable from 50m to 5,000m.

rolesPermissions.title: Roles and permissions
rolesPermissions.desc: Promote trusted staff to managers and choose exactly which areas they can administer — employees, shifts, closures, leave, attendance — toggle each one on or off per manager. Settings, billing and sub-QR codes always stay with the owner.

basilbookIntegration.desc: Connect DailyBrew to your BasilBook accounting system. Link employees by username and let BasilBook pull attendance data via a secure API.

perDayShifts.title: Per-day shift schedules
perDayShifts.desc: Different hours on different days? Override shift times per day of the week. Monday 7-3, Saturday 9-5 — all on the same shift.

leaveRequests.title: Leave request management
leaveRequests.desc: Employees submit leave requests (paid or unpaid, full or partial day). Owners approve or reject with a note. Full history and status tracking.

notifications.title: Push & email notifications
notifications.desc: Real-time push notifications and email alerts for leave requests, approvals, shift changes, closures, and daily attendance summaries.

why.title: Why restaurant teams choose DailyBrew
why.subtitle: Built specifically for restaurants, cafes, and small teams.

reasons.noHardware: No hardware — just a printed QR code

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.subtitle: Free for up to 10 employees. No credit card required.

footer.brandTagline: Staff attendance and leave tracking for restaurants. Simple, warm, and built for teams that move fast.

hero.subtitle: One QR code at your restaurant. Staff scan to check in. You see who's here, who's late, and who's on leave — all in real time.

howItWorks.title: Up and running in minutes
howItWorks.subtitle: Four steps from sign-up to live attendance tracking. No hardware needed.
howItWorks.learnMore: Learn more about how it works

create.title: Create your workspace
create.desc: Sign up, name your restaurant, and a unique QR code is generated for your workspace instantly.

addTeam.title: Add your team
addTeam.desc: Create employee profiles and assign shifts. Morning, evening, or custom — you define the schedule.

scan.title: Staff scan to check in
scan.desc: Display the QR code at your restaurant. Employees scan it to check in and out — one tap each way.

track.title: Track everything live
track.desc: See who is present, late, on leave, or absent. Late arrivals and early departures are flagged automatically.

features.title: Everything your restaurant needs
features.subtitle: Built for restaurant owners who want clarity without complexity.

qrCheckin.title: QR check-in
qrCheckin.desc: One QR code per workspace. Staff scan it to check in and out — fast, contactless, no extra hardware.

employeeManagement.title: Employee management
employeeManagement.desc: Add staff, assign shifts, and track attendance history. Manage active and inactive employees.

shiftTracking.title: Shift tracking
shiftTracking.desc: Define morning, evening, or custom shifts. Late arrivals and early departures are flagged automatically.

realtimeDashboard.title: Real-time dashboard
realtimeDashboard.desc: See who's present, late, on leave, or absent. Live stats for owners and employees.

leaveRequests.title: Leave requests
leaveRequests.desc: Staff submit leave from their phone. Owners approve or reject with one tap. Full or partial day.

ipRestriction.title: IP restriction
ipRestriction.desc: Lock check-ins to your restaurant's WiFi. Prevent remote punching.

deviceVerification.title: Device verification
deviceVerification.desc: Bind check-in/out to one device per employee per day. Prevents buddy punching.

geofencing.title: Geofencing
geofencing.desc: Restrict check-ins to a GPS radius around your restaurant.

perDaySchedules.title: Per-day schedules
perDaySchedules.desc: Set different shift hours for each day of the week.

managerRole.title: Manager role
managerRole.desc: Promote trusted staff and pick exactly which areas they administer — five granular permissions, configurable per manager.

basilbookIntegration.desc: Sync staff attendance to your accounting system. Link employees by username and pull data via API.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.desc: Push and email alerts for leave requests, shift changes, closures, and daily summaries.

builtFor.title: Any team that needs attendance tracking
builtFor.subtitle: Whether you run a single cafe or a busy restaurant with 20 staff, DailyBrew adapts to your workflow.

integration.subtitle: Connect staff attendance to your restaurant accounting. Know who worked, when they worked, and how it maps to your financials.

sync.title: Attendance synced to accounting
sync.desc: Pulls attendance data via API — match who worked with what was sold.

link.title: Username-based staff linking
link.desc: Link employees across both systems with a shared username. One identity, two products.

period.title: Period-based data retrieval
period.desc: Query attendance by date range — daily, weekly, or monthly. Up to 93 days per request.

howItWorks.label: How it works:
howItWorks.body: Generate an API token in DailyBrew settings. <brand></brand> uses it to pull attendance data for employees with matching usernames — check-in times, late flags, and shift info.

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.subtitle: Have questions? We'd love to hear from you.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.body: Use the feedback widget in the bottom-right corner to report bugs or suggest features. We read every message.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Upgrade when you need more.

features.manager: Manager role with granular permissions (up to 2)

pricing.footnote: No credit card required to start. Upgrade anytime from your dashboard.

owner.title: Owner setup
owner.subtitle: From sign-up to live attendance, in about 10 minutes.
owner.teaser: Create your workspace, define shifts, add staff, and start tracking.

createAccount.title: Create your account
createAccount.desc: Sign up with email, Google, or Apple. No credit card needed.

nameRestaurant.title: Name your restaurant
nameRestaurant.desc: In the onboarding wizard, pick the owner role and give your workspace a name. Timezone auto-detects from your browser.

defineShifts.title: Define your shifts
defineShifts.desc: Console → Shifts. Add Morning, Evening, or any custom shift with start and end times.

addEmployees.title: Add your employees
addEmployees.desc: Console → Employees → Add. Fill in name and assign a shift. Linking to a user account is optional now — you can do it later from the employee detail page.

linkUserAccount.title: Link an employee to a user account
linkUserAccount.desc: Open the employee → Link user card → paste the user's public ID (they can copy theirs from Console → Profile). Once linked, the user signs in to see their own attendance, submit leave, and check in via the app. Unlink anytime from the same card.

displayQr.title: Display the workspace QR
displayQr.desc: Your dashboard shows the QR code. Print it and pin it at the staff entrance — one QR for the whole restaurant.

watchLive.title: Watch attendance live
watchLive.desc: As staff check in, your dashboard updates with present, late, on leave, and absent counts.

approveLeaveClosures.title: Approve leave and schedule closures
approveLeaveClosures.desc: Review leave requests on the Leave tab. Add closures for holidays so staff are not marked absent.

employee.title: Employee day-to-day
employee.subtitle: Check in, check out, and request time off from your phone.
employee.teaser: Install the app, link to your workspace, and scan the QR to clock in.

installApp.title: Install the DailyBrew app
installApp.desc: Available free on the App Store and Google Play.

signIn.title: Sign in
signIn.desc: Use email, Google, or Apple — the same account works on every phone you sign in from.

linkRecord.title: Link to your employee record
linkRecord.desc: Two ways: (a) your owner pastes your user public ID — find it on Console → Profile — into the Link user card on your employee page; or (b) you paste the employee public ID your owner shares into the onboarding wizard or Console → Profile → Link to a workspace. You can link to several workspaces if you work at more than one restaurant.

scanQr.title: Scan the QR at the restaurant
scanQr.desc: Point the app at the QR on the wall. The check-in screen opens automatically.

tapCheckin.title: Tap Check in
tapCheckin.desc: On-time or Late status appears immediately, based on your assigned shift.

tapCheckout.title: Tap Check out at the end of your shift
tapCheckout.desc: Scan the same QR and tap Check out. If device verification is on, you must use the same phone you checked in with.

submitLeave.title: Submit leave from the Leave tab
submitLeave.desc: Pick the dates and type (paid or unpaid). Your owner gets notified and reviews it.

espresso.title: Upgrade to Espresso
espresso.subtitle: Unlock leave management, geofencing, managers, and BasilBook integration.
espresso.teaser: Lock check-in to your restaurant, promote managers, and sync with BasilBook.

openSettings.title: Open Settings → Subscription
openSettings.desc: From your dashboard, click Settings, then Upgrade in the Subscription card.

pickBilling.title: Pick monthly or yearly
pickBilling.desc: Monthly is $14.99. Yearly is $149 — about 17% cheaper. A 14-day free trial covers your first run.

lockCheckin.title: Lock check-in to your restaurant
lockCheckin.desc: Settings → enable IP restriction (use the "Use my current IP" button) or set a geofence with latitude, longitude, and radius (default 100 m).

enableDeviceVerification.title: Turn on device verification
enableDeviceVerification.desc: Forces each employee to use the same phone for both check-in and check-out, preventing buddy punching.

tryNfc.title: Try NFC check-in (optional)
tryNfc.desc: Replace the QR scan with a one-second tap against an NFC sticker. NTAG213 tags cost about a dollar each and program in 30 seconds with a free app.

promoteManager.title: Promote a manager
promoteManager.desc: Employee detail → set role to manager. Managers approve leave and see all attendance, up to 2 per workspace on Espresso.

connectBasilbook.title: Connect BasilBook
connectBasilbook.desc: Settings → API tokens → Generate. Copy the token once and paste it into BasilBook to sync attendance with your accounting.

nfc.title: Set up NFC check-in
nfc.subtitle: Replace the QR scan with a one-second tap against an NFC sticker.
nfc.teaser: Buy stickers, program them with your workspace URL, place them at the counter — staff tap their phone and they're in.

checkEspresso.title: Make sure you're on Espresso
checkEspresso.desc: NFC check-in is included in Espresso along with geofencing, device verification, and managers. Free plans see a "Espresso" badge next to the NFC toggle.

turnOnNfc.title: Turn on NFC check-in
turnOnNfc.desc: Console → Settings → "NFC check-in" card → flip the toggle, then Save. This unlocks the "Tap NFC tag" button on each employee's home screen in the mobile app.

copyUrl.title: Copy your check-in URL
copyUrl.desc: Console → Dashboard → "Check-in QR code" card. Under the QR you'll see a "For NFC tags" row with the URL — click the copy icon. The URL looks like https://dailybrew.work/checkin/<your-token>. Each workspace has its own URL, so if you run multiple restaurants copy each one separately.

buyStickers.title: Buy NTAG213 NFC stickers
buyStickers.desc: NTAG213 holds 137 bytes — plenty for our URL — and runs about $0.50–$1 each. Search "NTAG213 sticker" on Amazon or Lazada. Get round stickers for the host stand, PVC cards for the till, or key fobs for managers. NTAG215 (504 bytes) works too if you want headroom.

installApp.title: Install a free tag-writing app
installApp.desc: On the phone you'll use to program the tags, install "NFC Tools" (wakdev, free on iOS and Android) or NXP "TagWriter" on Android. You only need this once.

writeUrl.title: Write the URL onto a sticker
writeUrl.desc: In NFC Tools: Write tab → Add a record → URL/URI → paste the URL you copied → OK → Write. Hold your phone against a blank sticker. You'll feel a buzz in 1–2 seconds when it's done. Repeat for each sticker.

lockStickers.title: Lock the stickers (recommended)
lockStickers.desc: In NFC Tools: Other → Lock tag. This makes the sticker read-only forever so a staff member can't accidentally — or intentionally — overwrite it. Skip if you might re-program later.

placeStickers.title: Stick them where staff naturally pass
placeStickers.desc: Front of the host stand, under the bar lip, beside the time-clock area. The phone needs to be within 1–4 cm of the sticker, so avoid putting it behind glass or thick wood. Adding a small "Tap to check in" label next to it removes confusion on day one.

testTap.title: Test the tap
testTap.desc: Lock your phone (or close the DailyBrew app). Tap the sticker. On iPhone XS or newer, a banner appears at the top — tap it. On older iPhones, open the app first then tap. Android handles both automatically. After Face ID or passcode, you'll see "Checked in".

pairSecurity.title: Pair with geofence / IP / device verification
pairSecurity.desc: NFC by itself only proves the phone touched the tag — pair it with your geofence radius, allowed IP list, or device verification (already in Settings) so a stolen tag image or a copied tag can't be used from off-site.

index.title: Pick your path
index.subtitle: Short, focused playbooks for the three things people ask us about most. Each one takes about five minutes to read.
index.bigPicturePrefix: Looking for the big picture instead?

howItWorks.title: Up and running in minutes
howItWorks.subtitle: Four simple steps from sign-up to live attendance tracking. No hardware, no complex setup.

create.title: Create your workspace
create.desc: Sign up and name your restaurant. DailyBrew generates a unique QR code for your workspace automatically — print it and display it at your entrance.

addTeam.title: Add your employees
addTeam.desc: Create employee profiles with names and phone numbers. Assign each person to a shift so DailyBrew knows when to expect them.
details: Assign morning, evening, or custom shifts
Set shift start and end times
Optionally link employees to user accounts

scan.title: Staff scan to check in
scan.desc: Employees open the DailyBrew app on their phone, scan the workspace QR code, and they are checked in. Same scan to check out at the end of their shift.
details: One tap to check in, one tap to check out
Optional IP, device, and geofence verification

track.title: Track everything live
track.desc: Your dashboard shows who is present, who is late, who is on leave, and who has not shown up — all in real time. Late arrivals and early departures are flagged automatically.

perspectives.title: Built for owners and employees
perspectives.subtitle: Restaurant owners get a management dashboard. Employees get a simple mobile experience.

features.security: Configure IP, device, and geofence rules
features.notifications: Daily summary via push and email

features.qrScan: Scan QR to check in and out
features.history: View own shift and attendance history
features.shiftAlerts: Get notified of shift changes and closures
features.approvalStatus: See approval status in real time

espressoTeaser.title: Ready for geofencing, managers, and BasilBook?

cta.title: Ready to simplify attendance?
cta.subtitle: Free for up to 10 employees. No credit card required.

comparison.title: Feature comparison
comparison.subtitle: See exactly what you get with each plan.

switchPlans.question: Can I switch plans anytime?
switchPlans.answer: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time from your dashboard Settings. Changes take effect immediately.

paymentMethods.question: What payment methods do you accept?
paymentMethods.answer: We use Paddle as our payment provider, which supports credit cards, debit cards, and PayPal.

freeTrial.question: Is there a free trial?
freeTrial.answer: The Free plan is free forever with no time limit. If you want to try Espresso, you get a 14-day free trial with full access to all Espresso features.

cancelAnytime.question: Can I cancel anytime?
cancelAnytime.answer: Yes, there are no contracts or lock-in periods. You can cancel your Espresso subscription at any time and continue using the Free plan.

basilbook.question: How does the BasilBook integration work?
basilbook.answer: With an Espresso or Double Espresso plan, you can generate an API token from your workspace settings. BasilBook uses this token to pull attendance data for employees that share a username across both systems. The API returns check-in/out times, late/early flags, and shift info for any date range.

support.title: How can we help?
support.subtitle: Send us a message and we'll get back to you within 24 hours.

errors.name: Please enter your name
errors.subject: Please enter a subject
errors.message: Message must be at least 10 characters

success.title: Message sent
success.subtitle: We'll get back to you within 24 hours.

toasts.invalidType: Only PNG, JPEG, WebP, and GIF images are allowed
toasts.sendFailed: Failed to send message. Please try again.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to the most common questions.

emailFallback.title: Prefer email?
emailFallback.subtitle: You can also reach us directly at the address below.

roles.title: Who can do what
roles.subtitle: DailyBrew has three roles — owner, manager, and employee. Each with clear, well-defined permissions.

owner.title: Owner
owner.desc: The user who created the workspace. Full control over everything — employees, shifts, closures, settings, billing, and all attendance data.
highlights: Configures all settings and security rules

manager.title: Manager
manager.desc: A trusted employee promoted by the owner. The owner picks exactly which areas the manager can administer — no all-or-nothing access.
highlights: Per-manager permission toggles for employees, shifts, closures, leave, and attendance
Defaults to "manage leave" + "manage attendance" on promotion
Workspace settings, billing, sub-QRs, and promoting managers stay owner-only
Still checks in/out like a regular employee

employee.title: Employee
employee.desc: A staff member linked to the workspace. Can check in, view personal data, and submit leave requests.
highlights: Checks in and out by scanning the QR code
Submits leave requests (full or partial day)
Views own shift and closure info

matrix.title: Permissions matrix
matrix.subtitle: Every action mapped to every role. No surprises.

actions.checkinOut: Check in and check out via QR

voter.title: How permissions work under the hood
voter.subtitle: DailyBrew uses four permission levels. Every API request is checked against these before any action is taken.

view.label: VIEW
view.desc: Read-only access to workspace resources. All linked users get this.

capability.label: CAPABILITY
capability.desc: Granular attributes — manage_employees, manage_shifts, manage_closures, manage_leave, manage_attendance — granted individually to each manager. The owner has every capability implicitly.

editWorkspace.label: EDIT / DELETE on Workspace
editWorkspace.desc: Rename or delete the workspace, edit settings, manage billing, mint sub-QR codes, promote managers.

perQrManager.label: PER-QR MANAGER
perQrManager.desc: Adds attendance + leave authority over the QR's assigned employees, on top of any workspace-wide permissions they hold.

note.label: Note:
note.body: Granting a capability is a deliberate choice per manager. A manager with manage_leave only can approve leave but won't see the employees list as editable. A manager with everything but manage_attendance still only sees their own attendance. The voter enforces this uniformly across every API endpoint.

cta.title: Ready to set up your team?
cta.subtitle: Create your workspace, add employees, and promote your trusted staff.

demo.title: Try DailyBrew
demo.subtitle: Sign in with any of the three demo accounts below to explore DailyBrew from different perspectives.

owner.desc: Full access to everything — dashboard, employees, shifts, closures, settings, billing, and leave approvals.

manager.desc: A trusted employee with elevated permissions — can oversee attendance and handle leave requests.
highlights: Check in and out like a regular employee
No access to settings or employee management

employee.desc: A staff member — can check in, view their own attendance, and submit leave requests.
highlights: View own shift and check-in history
Submit leave requests (full or partial day)
Check in and out via QR code

ipRestriction.title: IP restriction
ipRestriction.desc: Lock check-ins to your restaurant's WiFi. Prevent remote punching.

deviceVerification.title: Device verification
deviceVerification.desc: Bind check-in/out to one device per employee per day. Prevents buddy punching.

geofencing.title: Geofencing
geofencing.desc: Restrict check-ins to a GPS radius around your restaurant.

cta.subtitle: Start free, upgrade to Espresso when you're ready.

deviceVerification.title: Device verification
deviceVerification.subtitle: Each employee must check in and check out from the same device on the same day. No more colleagues clocking out for each other — the system knows which phone checked in.

problem.lead: "Buddy punching" is one of the most common attendance fraud issues in restaurants.
problem.p1: Employee A leaves early but asks Employee B to check them out at the end of the shift. Or one person uses multiple phones to check in for absent colleagues. Without device verification, there's no way to tell who is actually scanning the QR code — you just see a check-in time, not which device was used.
problem.p2: In a busy restaurant, owners can't watch the door all day. Device verification does that job automatically.

uniqueId.title: Every device gets a unique ID
uniqueId.desc: When an employee opens DailyBrew on their phone for the first time, the app generates a unique device ID and stores it locally. This ID is invisible to the employee — it works silently in the background, no setup required on their end.

lockDevice.title: Check-in locks to that device
lockDevice.desc: When an employee checks in, DailyBrew records which device was used. The device ID and device name (parsed from the browser user agent) are stored on the attendance record. This is the device they must use to check out later.

oneDevice.title: One device per employee, one employee per device
oneDevice.desc: If a second employee tries to check in using the same phone that someone else already used today, the system blocks it. And if an employee tries to check out from a different device than the one they checked in with, that's blocked too.

auditTrail.title: Full audit trail on every record
auditTrail.desc: Each attendance record stores both the check-in and check-out device IDs along with the parsed device names (e.g., "iPhone 15 Pro, Safari 17"). Owners can see exactly which device was used for each clock event.

eliminate.title: Eliminate buddy punching
eliminate.desc: A colleague can't check out for someone who already left. The check-out must come from the same device that checked in — no exceptions.

onePhone.title: One phone, one person
onePhone.desc: A single phone can't be used to check in multiple employees on the same day. Each device is bound to one employee per day.

audit.title: Transparent audit trail
audit.desc: Every attendance record shows the device ID and name for both check-in and check-out. If there's ever a dispute, you have the data.

frictionless.title: Zero friction for employees
frictionless.desc: No apps to install, no codes to enter, no fingerprints to scan. The device ID is generated automatically in the browser. Employees don't even know it's there.

setup.subtitle: The simplest security feature to enable — just one toggle. No configuration needed on employee devices.

openSettings.title: Go to workspace settings
openSettings.desc: Open your dashboard and click "Settings" in the sidebar. You'll see a "Device verification" section.

enable.title: Enable device verification
enable.desc: Toggle on "Enable device verification". If you're on the free plan, you'll be prompted to upgrade to Espresso first.

save.title: Save — that's it
save.desc: Hit save. The system immediately starts enforcing device binding. The next time any employee checks in, their device ID is recorded and locked for the day. No action needed from your staff.

normal.label: Normal check-in:
normal.desc: nothing changes. They scan the QR code on their own phone and it works exactly as before. The device binding happens invisibly.

wrongDevice.label: Wrong device on check-out:
wrongDevice.desc: if they try to check out from a different phone, they see a clear message: "You must use the same device you checked in with." No cryptic errors.

alreadyUsed.label: Device already used:
alreadyUsed.desc: if Employee B tries to check in on Employee A's phone (which A already used today), the system blocks it with a message explaining the device has already been used by another employee.

newPhone.q: What if an employee gets a new phone?
newPhone.a: Device binding is per day. When they check in on a new phone the next day, it becomes their device for that day. There's no long-term device registration — each day is a fresh start.

clearedData.q: What if an employee clears their browser data?
clearedData.a: Clearing browser data removes the stored device ID. A new ID will be generated next time they open the app. This means check-out could be blocked that day if the ID doesn't match the one from check-in. In practice, employees rarely clear browser data mid-shift.

shared.q: Does this work on shared or kiosk devices?
shared.a: Device verification is designed for employees using their own phones. If multiple people share one device (e.g., a tablet at the entrance), only the first person who checks in can use it that day. For shared devices, you may want to disable this feature and use IP restriction or geofencing instead.

whichDevice.q: Can I see which device an employee used?
whichDevice.a: Yes. Each attendance record includes the device name (parsed from the browser user agent) for both check-in and check-out. You can see entries like "iPhone 15 Pro, Safari 17" or "Samsung Galaxy S24, Chrome 126" in the attendance detail.

noApp.q: Does this require employees to install an app?
noApp.a: No. Device verification works entirely through the web browser. The device ID is a UUID stored in localStorage — it's generated automatically the first time an employee opens DailyBrew. No app install, no permissions, no setup.

deviceVerification.espressoCallout: Device verification is included in the Espresso plan ($14.99/month or $149/year). Combine it with IP restriction and geofencing for complete check-in security.

geofencing.title: Geofencing
geofencing.subtitle: Draw a virtual boundary around your restaurant. When employees try to check in, DailyBrew verifies their GPS location — if they're outside the radius, the check-in is blocked.

problem.lead: IP restriction depends on network — but not every restaurant has a reliable, static WiFi setup.
problem.p1: Some restaurants share a building's WiFi. Some use mobile hotspots that change IPs. And some staff use mobile data instead of WiFi. In these situations, IP restriction alone isn't enough to verify someone is actually on-site.
problem.p2: Geofencing solves this by using the one thing that can't be faked easily: the employee's physical GPS location.

center.title: You set a center point
center.desc: In your workspace settings, you define the GPS coordinates of your restaurant. The easiest way: click "Use my current location" while standing in the restaurant, and DailyBrew will auto-detect your latitude and longitude. You can also enter coordinates manually if you prefer.

radius.title: You choose a radius
radius.desc: Set how far from the center point employees are allowed to be — in meters. The default is 100m, which works for most restaurants. A small cafe might use 50m (the minimum). A large venue or outdoor seating area might need 200–500m. Maximum is 5,000m.

sharesLocation.title: Employee's phone shares its location
sharesLocation.desc: When an employee taps "Check in", their browser asks for location permission (a standard browser prompt). The GPS coordinates are sent with the check-in request. This happens once per check-in — DailyBrew does not track location continuously.

compares.title: DailyBrew compares distance
compares.desc: The server calculates the distance between the employee's GPS position and your restaurant's center point. If they're within the radius — check-in succeeds. If they're outside — the check-in is blocked with a clear error message.

verifyPresence.title: Verify physical presence
verifyPresence.desc: The most direct proof that an employee is at the restaurant. GPS doesn't depend on which WiFi network they're on — it confirms their actual location.

flexibleRadius.title: Flexible radius
flexibleRadius.desc: From 50m for a small cafe to 5,000m for a large property. Adjust the boundary to match your actual venue size, including outdoor areas and parking.

layered.title: Layer with other security
layered.desc: Use geofencing alone or combine it with IP restriction and device verification. When all three are active, an employee must be on the right network, using their own device, and physically at the restaurant.

onePrompt.title: One-time location prompt
onePrompt.desc: The browser only asks for location permission once. After that, check-ins are seamless. DailyBrew never tracks employees between check-ins — location is read once, verified, and not stored.

radiusGuide.title: Choosing the right radius
radiusGuide.intro: GPS accuracy varies by device, weather, and building density. Indoors, GPS can drift 5–20 meters. Choose a radius that covers your venue with a comfortable margin:

cafe.note: Tight boundary. Works well for small, single-floor locations with good GPS signal.

standard.note: Recommended for most restaurants. Covers indoor areas, kitchen, storage rooms, and a small patio.

large.note: For restaurants with large outdoor seating, rooftop areas, or spread across a compound.

campus.note: For properties with multiple buildings, large grounds, or locations where staff work across a wide area.

setup.subtitle: Best done while you're at the restaurant, so the auto-detect picks up the right coordinates.

openSettings.title: Go to workspace settings
openSettings.desc: Open your dashboard and click "Settings" in the sidebar. Scroll down to the "Geofencing" section.

enable.title: Enable geofencing
enable.desc: Toggle on "Enable geofencing for check-in". If you're on the free plan, you'll be prompted to upgrade to Espresso first.

setLocation.title: Set your restaurant's location
setLocation.desc: Click "Use my current location". Your browser will ask for permission to access your location — tap Allow. DailyBrew reads your GPS coordinates and fills in the latitude and longitude fields automatically. You can also enter coordinates manually if you already know them.

setRadius.title: Set the radius
setRadius.desc: Enter the radius in meters. Default is 100m. The minimum is 50m (GPS isn't accurate enough below this). For most restaurants, 100–200m works well. If you have outdoor seating or a large property, go wider.

saveVerify.title: Save and verify
saveVerify.desc: Hit save. You'll see a confirmation showing your coordinates and the active radius. Try a test check-in from the restaurant to make sure it works. Then try from across the street to verify it blocks — you'll see the boundary in action.

trackAllDay.q: Does DailyBrew track my employees' location all day?
trackAllDay.a: No. DailyBrew reads the GPS location once — at the moment of check-in (and again at check-out). The coordinates are used only to verify distance from the restaurant. DailyBrew does not store employee GPS data, does not track movement, and does not run in the background.

denied.q: What if the employee denies the location permission?
denied.a: If the employee blocks location access in their browser, the check-in request won't include GPS data and the geofence check will fail. They'll see a message asking them to allow location access. This is a standard browser permission — not a DailyBrew-specific app.

indoorAccuracy.a: Indoors, GPS accuracy can degrade to 10–20 meters depending on the building. That's why the minimum radius is 50m — anything smaller would cause too many false rejections. If your restaurant is in a building with weak GPS signal (basement, thick walls), consider using a slightly larger radius like 150–200m.

movedRemodel.q: What if my restaurant moves or I remodel?
movedRemodel.a: Just update the coordinates in settings. Click "Use my current location" from the new spot and save. The change takes effect immediately for all future check-ins.

fakeGps.q: Can employees fake their GPS location?
fakeGps.a: It's technically possible to spoof GPS on a rooted/jailbroken phone or with developer tools. However, this requires deliberate effort and technical knowledge. For the vast majority of restaurant staff, geofencing is an effective deterrent. For maximum security, combine geofencing with IP restriction and device verification.

iosAndroid.q: Does this work on both iOS and Android?
iosAndroid.a: Yes. Geofencing uses the standard browser Geolocation API, which is supported on all modern iPhones and Android phones. The browser handles the GPS reading — DailyBrew just receives the coordinates.

tip.title: Best practice: layer all three
tip.intro: Each security feature covers a different angle. Together, they make check-in fraud nearly impossible:

ipRestriction.label: IP restriction
ipRestriction.desc: Verifies the network — are they on restaurant WiFi?

deviceVerification.label: Device verification
deviceVerification.desc: Verifies the device — is this their own phone?

geofencing.label: Geofencing
geofencing.desc: Verifies the location — are they physically here?

geofencing.espressoCallout: Geofencing is included in the Espresso plan ($14.99/month or $149/year) alongside IP restriction, device verification, leave management, manager roles, and more.

ipRestriction.title: IP restriction
ipRestriction.subtitle: Make sure your staff are physically at the restaurant when they check in. IP restriction locks attendance to your restaurant's network — if they're not on your WiFi, they can't clock in.

problem.lead: Without IP restriction, any employee with the QR code data can check in from anywhere.
problem.p1: They could be at home, on the bus, or at another location entirely. If someone shares the QR payload with a colleague, that person can check in without ever stepping foot in the restaurant. You'd never know unless you were watching the door yourself.

publicIp.title: Your network has a public IP address
publicIp.desc: Every internet connection has a public IP address assigned by your ISP. When your staff are connected to your restaurant's WiFi, all their traffic goes through that IP. DailyBrew sees this IP on every check-in request.

tellTrusted.title: You tell DailyBrew which IPs to trust
tellTrusted.desc: In your workspace settings, you add one or more IP addresses. These are the only networks your staff are allowed to check in from. Any check-in attempt from a different IP is blocked with a clear error message.

invisibleValidation.title: Staff check in as normal — the validation is invisible
invisibleValidation.desc: Your employees don't need to do anything differently. They scan the QR code the same way they always do. If they're on the right network, it works. If not, they see a message explaining that check-in is restricted to the restaurant network.

preventRemote.title: Prevent remote check-ins
preventRemote.desc: Employees can't check in from home, the parking lot, or anywhere outside your restaurant. They must be connected to an approved network.

multipleNetworks.title: Support multiple networks
multipleNetworks.desc: Have a main WiFi and a backup connection? Add both IPs. You can list as many allowed IPs as you need — WiFi, ethernet, mobile hotspot.

failSafe.title: Fail-safe by design
failSafe.desc: If you enable IP restriction but forget to add any IPs, DailyBrew allows all check-ins instead of locking everyone out. No accidental lockouts.

anySetup.title: Works with any network setup
anySetup.desc: Static IP, dynamic IP, VPN — if the request arrives from an IP on your list, it's accepted. Works with any ISP or network configuration.

setup.subtitle: Takes less than a minute. You just need to be at the restaurant, connected to the WiFi you want to lock check-ins to.

openSettings.title: Go to workspace settings
openSettings.desc: Open your dashboard, click "Settings" in the sidebar. Scroll to the "Workspace settings" section.

enable.title: Enable IP restriction
enable.desc: Toggle on "Enable IP restriction". If you're on the free plan, you'll be prompted to upgrade to Espresso first.

addIp.title: Add your restaurant's IP
addIp.desc: Click the "Use my current IP" button. DailyBrew will detect your public IP address as seen by the server and add it to the list automatically. You can also type IPs manually — one per line — if you have additional networks.

save.title: Save your settings
save.desc: Hit "Save". From this moment, every check-in request is validated against your IP list. Employees on a different network will see a clear message telling them check-in is restricted.

ipChanges.q: What if my restaurant's IP address changes?
ipChanges.a: Some ISPs assign dynamic IPs that can change periodically. If that happens, your staff will be blocked until you update the IP. Just go to Settings, click "Use my current IP" again, and save. If your IP changes frequently, consider asking your ISP for a static IP or using geofencing as an alternative.

multipleIps.q: Can I allow multiple IP addresses?
multipleIps.a: Yes. You can add as many IPs as you need — one per line. This is useful if you have multiple network connections (main WiFi, backup ethernet, manager hotspot) or multiple restaurant locations under one workspace.

blockedMessage.q: What does the employee see when they're blocked?
blockedMessage.a: They see a warm amber warning message explaining that check-in is restricted to the restaurant network. No cryptic error codes — just a clear explanation that they need to be on the right WiFi.

combineSecurity.q: Can I use IP restriction together with geofencing and device verification?
combineSecurity.a: Absolutely. All three security features work independently and can be combined for layered protection. An employee would need to pass all enabled checks to successfully check in.

emptyList.q: What happens if I enable IP restriction but don't add any IPs?
emptyList.a: DailyBrew treats an empty IP list as a misconfiguration and allows all check-ins. This is a safety net — you won't accidentally lock out your entire team.

ipRestriction.espressoCallout: IP restriction is included in the Espresso plan ($14.99/month or $149/year). It also includes device verification, geofencing, leave management, manager roles, and more.

basilbookIntegration.subtitle: Connect DailyBrew to your BasilBook accounting system. Link employees by username, generate a secure API key, and let BasilBook pull attendance data automatically — check-in times, late flags, shift info, all in one call.

problem.lead: Attendance data lives in DailyBrew. Accounting data lives in BasilBook. Neither talks to the other.
problem.p1: Without integration, you'd have to manually cross-reference who was present, who was late, and what shifts were worked — then enter that into your accounting system by hand. That's error-prone, slow, and nobody wants to do it.
problem.p2: The BasilBook integration bridges the gap. BasilBook can pull attendance data directly from DailyBrew via a secure API, matching staff by username.

linkUsername.title: Link employees by username
linkUsername.desc: Each employee in DailyBrew can have a username — this is the same identifier used in BasilBook for that staff member. When BasilBook pulls attendance data, it matches records by this username.

generateToken.title: Generate a secure API token
generateToken.desc: In your workspace settings, generate an API token. The full token is shown once — copy it and store it in BasilBook. DailyBrew only stores a hash, so the plain token can never be retrieved again.

callApi.title: BasilBook calls the attendance API
callApi.desc: BasilBook sends a GET request with the API key in the X-Api-Key header, specifying a date range (up to 93 days). DailyBrew returns attendance records for all employees with a username — check-in/out times, late flags, shift info, all formatted in your workspace timezone.

flowsAuto.title: Data flows automatically
flowsAuto.desc: Once configured, BasilBook can pull attendance data on any schedule — daily, weekly, or on demand. No manual exports, no CSV files, no copy-pasting. The data is always fresh and formatted for BasilBook's needs.

apiReturns.title: What the API returns
apiReturns.intro: For each employee with a username, the API returns their attendance records within the requested date range:
apiReturns.footnote: Days with no attendance record are omitted — absence is implied by missing dates. Maximum date range per request: 93 days.

checkOutTime.label: Check-out time
checkOutTime.desc: HH:mm, or null if still working

lateFlag.label: Late flag
lateFlag.desc: true if arrived after shift start

leftEarlyFlag.label: Left early flag
leftEarlyFlag.desc: true if left before shift end

noManualEntry.title: No manual data entry
noManualEntry.desc: Attendance data flows from DailyBrew to BasilBook automatically. No spreadsheets, no copy-pasting, no errors from manual transcription.

alwaysFresh.title: Always up to date
alwaysFresh.desc: BasilBook pulls live data from DailyBrew. Whether it queries daily or weekly, the data reflects the latest check-ins, late flags, and shift assignments.

secure.title: Secure by design
secure.desc: API tokens are hashed on storage — the plain token is shown once and never stored. Tokens can be revoked instantly. Each token is scoped to one workspace.

usernameMatching.title: Username-based matching
usernameMatching.desc: Staff are linked by username across both systems. No fragile email matching or manual ID mapping. Set the username once and the link works automatically.

setup.subtitle: Two parts: link your employees by username, then generate an API token for BasilBook.

setUsernames.title: Set employee usernames
setUsernames.desc: Go to each employee's detail page and enter their BasilBook username. This must match the staff name or ID used in your BasilBook system exactly.

generateToken.title: Generate an API token
generateToken.desc: Go to Settings, scroll to "API tokens", and click "Generate token". Give it a name (e.g., "BasilBook production"). The full token is shown once — copy it immediately.

configureBb.title: Configure BasilBook
configureBb.desc: In BasilBook, enter the API token in the DailyBrew integration settings. BasilBook will use it in the X-Api-Key header when pulling attendance data.

testConnection.title: Test the connection
testConnection.desc: Trigger a test pull from BasilBook. You should see attendance records for all employees with a username. If an employee is missing, check that their username is set in DailyBrew.

lostToken.q: What if I lose the API token?
lostToken.a: The plain token is only shown once at creation. If you lose it, revoke the old token and generate a new one. Update the new token in BasilBook.

multipleTokens.q: Can I have multiple API tokens?
multipleTokens.a: Yes. You can generate multiple tokens — for example, one for production and one for testing. Each token can be revoked independently.

noUsername.q: What if an employee doesn't have a username?
noUsername.a: They won't appear in the API response. Only employees with a username set are included. This is intentional — the username is the linking key between DailyBrew and BasilBook.

maxRange.a: 93 days per request. For longer periods, make multiple requests with consecutive date ranges.

oneWay.q: Does BasilBook push data to DailyBrew?
oneWay.a: No. The integration is one-way: BasilBook pulls attendance data from DailyBrew. DailyBrew does not receive or modify any BasilBook data.

timezone.q: What timezone are the times in?
timezone.a: All times in the API response are formatted in your workspace timezone (e.g., Asia/Phnom_Penh). The timezone is included in the response so BasilBook can interpret the times correctly.

basilbookIntegration.espressoCallout: BasilBook integration and API tokens are included in the Espresso plan ($14.99/month or $149/year). Employee usernames for cross-product linking are also Espresso-only.

relatedShort.ipRestriction: Lock check-ins to your restaurant's WiFi.
relatedShort.deviceVerification: Prevent buddy punching with device binding.

cta.title: Ready to connect your systems?
cta.subtitle: Start free, upgrade to Espresso when you need the integration.

threeFactorAttendance.title: Three-factor attendance
threeFactorAttendance.subtitle: The strongest check-in configuration in DailyBrew. Combine IP restriction, device verification, and geofencing — each layer covers a gap the others can't. To clock in, a staff member must be on the right network, on the right phone, and physically at the restaurant.

singleLayer.title: No single layer is bulletproof
singleLayer.desc: IP restriction can be sidestepped by a shared building WiFi or a mobile hotspot. Device verification breaks if an employee clears their browser data. Geofencing can drift indoors. Each control has a known failure mode — so any single one, used alone, leaves a gap an employee can drive a colleague's check-in through.

complementary.title: The three controls complement each other
complementary.desc: IP confirms the network. Device verification confirms the identity of the phone doing the scanning. Geofencing confirms the physical body in the building. To bypass all three at once an employee would have to be on the restaurant WiFi, using their own registered phone, standing inside the geofence — which is the same thing as actually being at work.

auditable.title: Every layer leaves an audit trail
auditable.desc: Each attendance record stores the IP, both device IDs, the device names, and the GPS coordinates used for the check-in and check-out. If a dispute ever surfaces, owners have all three signals to compare — not one fuzzy data point.

ipRestriction.title: IP restriction — the network factor
ipRestriction.desc: Lock check-ins to the public IP addresses of your restaurant's WiFi or wired network. Any scan from outside that list — home, the bus, a parking lot — is blocked at the server with a clear message. Useful for venues with a stable ISP and known network footprint.

deviceVerification.title: Device verification — the identity factor
deviceVerification.desc: Each phone is silently assigned a unique ID the first time the employee opens DailyBrew. Check-in records that ID; check-out must come from the same device. A second person trying to clock in on a phone already used today is blocked. Eliminates buddy punching at the source.

geofencing.title: Geofencing — the location factor
geofencing.desc: You set the GPS coordinates of the restaurant and a radius (50–5,000m). At check-in the browser reads the employee's location once and the server verifies they're inside the perimeter. No background tracking — the coordinates are used to compare distance and then forgotten.

enable.title: How to enable all three
enable.subtitle: Best done while you're physically at the restaurant — the location auto-detect needs you on-site for accuracy.

openSettings.title: Open workspace settings
openSettings.desc: Sign in to your dashboard and click Settings in the sidebar. You'll see three separate cards: IP restriction, Device verification, and Geofencing.

enableThree.title: Turn on all three toggles
enableThree.desc: In IP restriction, add the restaurant's public IPs (use the "Use my current IP" button while on the restaurant WiFi). In Device verification, flip the toggle — no configuration needed. In Geofencing, use "Use my current location" to set the center, then pick a radius (100m works for most restaurants).

saveTest.title: Save and run a test scan
saveTest.desc: Hit Save on each card. Do a test check-in from inside the restaurant — it should succeed and show all three verification badges (network, device, location). Then try a check-in from across the street: it should fail with a clear message naming which check rejected it.

fallback.title: What happens when one layer fails
fallback.intro: Each layer is enforced independently. If any single check fails, the whole check-in is rejected — there's no partial success. That's deliberate: layered security means each control is a hard gate, not a weighted score.

ipDown.label: Restaurant WiFi goes down
ipDown.desc: Staff on a mobile hotspot will be blocked by IP restriction even if their device and GPS are fine. To recover: temporarily add the hotspot IP to the allow-list, or switch to manual attendance entry from the owner dashboard for the affected day.

newDevice.label: Employee gets a new phone
newDevice.desc: First check-in on the new phone will pass IP and geofence but device verification will treat it as a fresh device — they can check in but not yet check out from the old one. Device binding resets daily, so it self-heals on day two with no action needed.

gpsWeak.label: GPS drifts indoors
gpsWeak.desc: If GPS reads as 30m off inside a thick-walled building, geofencing may reject with a 100m radius. Pick a slightly wider radius (150–200m) to absorb indoor drift, or rely on IP + device verification as the two enforced layers for that workspace.

fallback.outro: Notifications fire on the first new-device check-in (Espresso) so owners catch a legitimate device change before it confuses anyone.

threeFactorAttendance.espressoCallout: All three layers — IP restriction, device verification, and geofencing — are included in the Espresso plan ($14.99/month or $149/year) alongside leave management, manager roles, and Telegram alerts.

cta.title: Ready to lock down your check-ins?

shift.description: Define your restaurant's working hours. Assign employees to shifts so DailyBrew can track late arrivals and early departures automatically.
shift.perDaySchedulesDesc: Override shift hours for specific days of the week. Available with Espresso.
shift.emptyHint: Click to create your first shift
